opinion.h: BAKER ET AL., DBA BAKER & FORD v. ALASKA. APPEAL FROM THE SUPREME COURT OF WASHINGTON. No. 849. Decided March 15, 1965.
opinion.p: Appeal dismissed for want of a substantial federal question.
Bruce T. Rinker, Charles S. Rhyne and Alfred J. Tighe, Jr., for appellants.
The appeal is dismissed for want of a substantial federal question. MR. JUSTICE BLACK and MR. JUSTICE DOUGLAS are of the opinion that probable jurisdiction should be noted.
380 U.S. 260 MARTINEZ v. UNITED STATES. ON PETITION FOR WRIT OF CERTIORARI TO THE UNITED STATES COURT OF APPEALS FOR THE NINTH CIRCUIT. No. 397, Misc. Decided March 15, 1965.
Certiorari granted, judgment vacated and case remanded.
Solicitor General Cox for the United States.

The motion for leave to proceed in forma pauperis and the petition for a writ of certiorari are granted. The judgment is vacated and the case is remanded to the United States District Court for the Northern District of California with instructions to make findings with respect to petitioner's consent to the search.
